The 3X VSL Formula
What Is “Vocal Tonality”?
Techniques for reading your VSL with vocal inflec+ons
that aid in the psychology of making the sale
These techniques include:
Punching
Elonga+on
Pre-­‐Recogni+on
Curiosity (The Whisper Effect)
The Strategic Pause
Everyman (Everywoman) Voicing

-- 2 of 16 --

The 3X VSL Formula
3X Vocal Tonality Technique
Punching
“Punching” a word simply means that you give it emphasis
over the other words on a given slide or slides
Example:
“The Real Truth Behind Success Is…”
versus
“The Real Truth Behind Success Is…”
In your text document, simply bold the words to punch

-- 3 of 16 --

The 3X VSL Formula
3X Vocal Tonality Technique
Punching
Words to consider punching include:
Power words (Buy, Now, Plus, Real, etc.)
Feeling words
However, there are no wrong answers as to which
words you decide to punch or elongate…this is up
to your style of reading and personal taste

-- 4 of 16 --

The 3X VSL Formula
3X Vocal Tonality Technique
Elonga-on
“ElongaMon” is a word that is enunciated slower than
other words on a slide to add dramaMc effect
Example:
“The Secret Is Called Caloric Staggering…”
versus
“The Secret Is Called Caloric Staggering…”
In your text document, simply underline the words to punch, and
you can combine techniques when you want
“The Secret Is Called Caloric Staggering…”

-- 5 of 16 --

The 3X VSL Formula
3X Vocal Tonality Technique
Elonga-on
Words to consider elongaMng:
Phrases specific to benefits
Complex or vital terms
Words prior to acMon words
(Really, Definitely, Specifically, etc.)

-- 6 of 16 --

The 3X VSL Formula
3X Vocal Tonality Technique
Pre-­‐Recogni-on
“Pre-­‐RecogniMon” is a fantasMc psychological sales technique
The idea is simple: If there’s ever a moment when you want to assume your
viewer should know something (especially when you introduce your
company or your brand), you enunciate everything as a ques+on
Example:
“I’m Jon Benson From 3X Formulas…”
versus
“I’m Jon Benson-­‐?-­‐ From 3X Formulas-­‐?-­‐”

-- 7 of 16 --

The 3X VSL Formula
3X Vocal Tonality Technique
Pre-­‐Recogni-on
Simply use this character at the end of any sentence
or phrase on which you wish to employ pre-­‐recogniMon
-­‐?-­‐
Use pre-­‐recogniMon when introducing yourself using
your full name (which comes during The Grand Offer)
and when introducing your product or service
The tone leads the viewer to think, “Oh… Right…”

-- 8 of 16 --

The 3X VSL Formula
3X Vocal Tonality Technique
Curiosity (The Whisper Effect)
“Curiosity” can be used when you use specific words like:
“Remember:”
“The Real Secret…”
“The Reason For This Is…”
Example:
“The Reason For This Is…”
versus
“The (Reason) For This Is…”

-- 9 of 16 --

The 3X VSL Formula
3X Vocal Tonality Technique
Curiosity (The Whisper Effect)
Simply put the word or phrase you wish to use in
parenthesis in your word document
(Remember:)
The psychology behind this trick is “the whisper
effect”—the idea that what you have to say is
“top secret” and should be “leant into”
The tone leads the viewer to think, “Hmm…”

-- 10 of 16 --

The 3X VSL Formula
3X Vocal Tonality Technique
The Strategic Pause
Simply pausing for a few seconds can serve as a
Pa]ern Interrupt if done in the right place, like:
A_er using the word, “Right?”, or “Deal?”
(Something that requires a split-­‐second agreement)
A_er making a very, very important statement
that you want to really sink in

-- 11 of 16 --

The 3X VSL Formula
3X Vocal Tonality Technique
The Strategic Pause
Example:
“Never Eat Starchy Carbs In The Morning… This Allows You…”
versus
“Never Eat Starchy Carbs In The Morning -­‐-­‐-­‐ This Allows You…”
Simply use three dashes to denote a pause (-­‐-­‐-­‐)
Allows the prospect to “digest” important info and
prompts him/her to agree psychologically

-- 12 of 16 --

The 3X VSL Formula
3X Vocal Tonality Technique
Everyman (Everywoman) Voicing
This simply means that you want to read your VSL
with a natural, smooth, conversa+onal-­‐style
tone, rather than being too rigid or to “perfect”
ImperfecMons in video sales le]ers can make you
come across as more genuine (but too many can
make you look like an amateur)

-- 13 of 16 --

The 3X VSL Formula
3X Vocal Tonality Technique
Everyman (Everywoman) Voicing
First-­‐Mme VSL recorders should try the following:
Read and record standing up
Consider recording only a few slides at a Mme
PracMce reading your VSL in front of friends/family
Ignore the “sound” of your own voice, and just
focus on the techniques and you’ll do great!

-- 14 of 16 --

The 3X VSL Formula
Conclusion
Do not feel the need to include these techniques on
every slide of your VSL — use each of them or
combinaMons in 10-­‐20 slides tops, and simply take the
Everyman Approach for the rest
Do not become obsessed over which word or phrase to
use a specific technique — the key to success is simply
finding the words that feel natural to you to
punch, elongate, etc.
Above all, have fun and be yourself when recording—
That will set you apart from just about every
sales page on the Internet!
